feedback.text: I think the Digital Fairness Act should include protections from the Stop Killing Games movement. It's not fair that people can buy a game and then lose access to it forever just because the servers get shut down. If you pay for something, you should be able to keep using it. Adding a rule that forces companies to offer offline modes or let players run their own servers when a game is shut down would go a long way toward making digital purchases more fair. Games shouldnt just disappear. It's not fair, not for anyone.
